questions.questionText: Transitional epithelium (urothelium) is characteristically found in the:
questions.explanation: Urothelium lines the urinary tract from renal calyces to proximal urethra.

questions.questionText: Pseudostratified ciliated columnar epithelium lines the:
questions.explanation: Respiratory epithelium lines most of the conducting airways.

questions.questionText: Which of the following cells produce myelin in the central nervous system?
questions.explanation: Oligodendrocytes myelinate CNS axons; Schwann cells myelinate PNS axons.

questions.questionText: The functional unit of compact bone is the:
questions.explanation: The osteon consists of concentric lamellae around a central canal.

questions.questionText: Features of cardiac muscle include all EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: Cardiac muscle cells typically have a single central nucleus.
Nissl substance is granular RER involved in protein synthesis.

questions.questionText: The principal cell type responsible for producing the extracellular matrix of cartilage is the:
questions.explanation: Chondrocytes secrete and maintain the cartilage matrix.

questions.questionText: Stratified squamous non-keratinized epithelium is found in the:
questions.explanation: Moist surfaces subject to friction like the esophagus and vagina have this.

questions.questionText: Which formed element of blood lacks a nucleus?
questions.explanation: RBCs lose their nucleus during maturation to maximize hemoglobin content.

questions.questionText: Hassall's corpuscles are a histological feature of the:
questions.explanation: Hassall's corpuscles are found in the thymic medulla.

questions.questionText: In Histology, which statement is most accurate regarding the core definition and diagnostic concept?
questions.optionA: It reflects the most exam-relevant definition used in standard Anatomy texts
questions.optionB: It is an obsolete definition no longer used in clinical practice
questions.optionC: It is used only in pediatric population and never in adults
questions.optionD: It has no diagnostic significance in NEET-PG pattern questions
questions.explanation: This tests foundational definition-level knowledge from standard Anatomy references for Histology. Subject: Anatomy. Chapter: Histology.

questions.questionText: The most important etiology/risk factor to suspect first in a typical Histology scenario is:
questions.optionA: The common high-yield risk factor repeatedly asked in entrance exams
questions.optionB: A rare cause seen only in isolated case reports
questions.optionD: An etiology excluded by routine first-line workup
questions.explanation: NEET-PG and INI-CET focus on common, high-yield causes before rare differentials. Subject: Anatomy. Chapter: Histology.
